players.injury_note: Black (quadriceps) participated in Thursday's morning shootaround, Jason Beede of the Orlando Sentinel reports.
Carter (ankle) participated in Thursday's morning shootaround, Jason Beede of the Orlando Sentinel reports.
Wagner will be sidelined indefinitely after recent tests showed that he requires additional time and rehabilitation for soreness in his left high ankle sprain, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Bagley is out for Thursday's game against Orlando.
Irving (knee) will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
Lively successfully underwent surgery on his right foot Sunday.
Marshall is probable for Thursday's game against Orlando with a right finger contusion.
Thompson is probable for Thursday's game against the Magic due to a right adductor contusion.
Williams (quadriceps) is questionable for Thursday's game against Orlando.
The Wizards announced Thursday that Davis (finger) will be re-evaluated in two weeks.
The Wizards announced Wednesday that George has been diagnosed with a partial tear of the UCL in his left elbow and will be re-evaluated in three weeks.
Russell (not injury related) has been ruled out for Tuesday's game against the Magic.
Sarr (hamstring) is out for Thursday's game against the Jazz.
Vukcevic (thigh) is out for Thursday's game against the Jazz.
Watkins (ankle) is out for Thursday's game against the Jazz.
Whitmore has begun the recovery process for a diagnosed venous condition and he will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season.
George is out for Thursday's game against the Wizards due to right ankle injury management.
Jackson was diagnosed with a localized PVNS growth in his left knee and will miss the remainder of the season, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
Kessler will undergo left shoulder surgery and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, Tony Jones of The Athletic reports.
Utah will re-evaluate Markkanen in two weeks after Thursday's MRI showed symptomatic hip impingement with associated inflammation and bone bruise, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Nurkic is scheduled to undergo season-ending nose surgery Wednesday, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
Williams has been diagnosed with a torn left ACL and will miss the remainder of the season, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Fontecchio has a left groin strain and will not play Thursday against the Nets.
Jovic (back) is out for Thursday's game against the Nets.
Powell is week-to-week with a right groin strain, per Anthony Chiang of the Miami Herald.
The NBA placed Rozier on immediate leave from the Heat on Thursday, ESPN's Shams Charania reports.
Demin (foot) won't play in Thursday's game against Miami, per C.J. Holmes of the New York Daily News.
The Rockets announced Wednesday that Adams has undergone season-ending left ankle surgery, per Shams Charania of ESPN.
Sengun is questionable to play Thursday versus the Warriors due to an illness.
Smith is questionable for Thursday's game against the Warriors with a right ankle sprain.
Tate has been diagnosed with a Grade 2 MCL sprain in his right knee and will be re-evaluated in two weeks, Vanessa Richardson of Space City Home Network reports.
Thompson (ankle) is listed as questionable for Thursday's game against the Warriors.
VanVleet suffered a torn ACL and may be out for the entire 2025-26 campaign, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Butler underwent successful surgery Monday to repair a torn right anterior cruciate ligament.
Cryer (hamstring) is out for Wednesday's game against the Spurs.
Curry (back) is out for Thursday's game against Houston.
Curry (knee) will be re-evaluated in 10 days, Anthony Slater of ESPN.com reports.
Moody (wrist) will not play Thursday against Golden State.
Payton (ankle) is questionable for Thursday's game against Houston.
Porzingis (illness) is out for Thursday's game against Houston.
Richard (ankle) will miss Thursday's game against Houston.
Clark (illness) is questionable for Thursday's game against Toronto.
Edwards (foot) is questionable for Thursday's game versus Toronto.
Ingles is out for Thursday's game against Toronto for personal reasons.
Hepburn (knee) was spotted Wednesday no longer using crutches, Ryan Wolstat of the Toronto Sun reports.
Ingram is questionable for Thursday's game against Minnesota with a left thumb sprain.
Murray-Boyles is out for Thursday's game against Minnesota with a left thumb sprain.
Barnes (ankle) is out for Thursday's game against the Pistons.
Ingram has signed a two-way contract with the Spurs, Michael Scotto of USA Today reports.
Jones Garcia underwent ankle surgery Wednesday and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, Tom Orsborn of the San Antonio Express-News reports.
Plumlee (reconditioning) is out for Thursday's game against the Pistons.
Brooks' timetable for his broken left hand is 4-to-6 weeks, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Goodwin (calf) will remain on the inactive list for Thursday's game against the Bulls.
Williams has been ruled out for Thursday's game against the Bulls due to left foot soreness.
Buzelis (ankle) is questionable for Thursday's game against the Suns.
Collins will undergo surgery for a sprain right toe and will be sidelined for the rest of the 2025-26 season.
Essengue will undergo left shoulder surgery and will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 season, K.C. Johnson of Chicago Sports Network reports.
Giddey (ankle) is questionable for Thursday's game against the Suns.
Ivey (knee) won't travel with the Bulls for their upcoming five-game road trip, K.C. Johnson of Chicago Sports Network reports.
Simons has been diagnosed with a fractured left wrist, Will Gottlieb of AllCHGO.com reports.
Smith (calf) is doubtful for Thursday's game against the Suns.
Williams (quadriceps) is doubtful for Thursday's game against the Suns.
Gordon (hamstring) is targeting a return Friday against the Knicks, Sam Amick of The Athletic reports.
Johnson (ankle) is questionable for Thursday's game versus the Lakers.
Jones (shoulder) has been ruled out for Thursday's game against the Lakers.

Watson has been ramping up his basketball activities as he continues to recover from a right hamstring strain and is slated to be re-examined later this week, Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints.com reports.
Cardwell sprained his left ankle at Tuesday's practice and will be re-evaluated in four weeks, James Ham of ESPN 1320 Sacramento reports.
Hunter is set to undergo season-ending eye surgery, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
LaVine will undergo season-ending surgery on his right hand after the All-Star break, Chris Haynes of NBA TV reports.
Murray will have his sprained left ankle reevaluated by medical staff in two weeks, Matt George of ABC 10 Sacramento reports.
Sabonis underwent season-ending knee surgery Wednesday to repair a torn left meniscus, Shams Charania of ESPN reports.
Murphy (neck) intends to play in Thursday's game against the Kings, per Mark Haynes of The Sacramento Observer.
Murray (reconditioning) is out for Thursday's game against the Kings.
Williamson (ankle) is questionable for Thursday's game in Sacramento.
